units.content: File is a dependency registry system with language-free core using integer IDs
File provides DependencyEntry class mapping integer IDs to human-readable descriptions
File provides GraphEvent class recording changes to graph as append-only history
File provides DEP registry of all dependencies used by inference chains
File includes vocabulary partition validator distinguishing operational vs theoretical enforcement
TheoreticalPartitionError raised when theoretical identifier registered into operational vocabulary
Vocabulary partition is enforced at schema level
Theoretical commitments, target assumptions, and framework claims belong outside dependency graph
All identifiers in inference-chain depends lists have operational=True
Operational dependencies name physical or computational mechanisms instrument embodies
LORENTZ_FORCE is marked operational=True
Penning trap literally IS a Lorentz-force device
LORENTZ_FORCE is operational because instrument physically embodies it
CENTRAL_DOGMA marked operational because CRISPR screen relies on DNA→RNA→protein
PLANCK_CMB_PREDICTION marked operational because pipeline literally runs this calculation
THERMODYNAMIC_EQUIL marked operational because incubators physically embody it at 37°C
COSMOLOGICAL_PRINCIPLE marked operational=False
COSMOLOGICAL_PRINCIPLE is theoretical because it is conceptual commitment not mechanism
LEPTON_UNIVERSALITY marked operational=False
LEPTON_UNIVERSALITY is theoretical because it is assumption about particle families not a mechanism
QFT framework marked operational=False
QFT is theoretical because it is a framework not a mechanism
DM_IS_SUBSTANCE marked operational=False
DM_IS_SUBSTANCE is theoretical because it is assumption about target not a mechanism
_validate_partition function raises error for non-operational entries
System enforces strict separation between operational and theoretical entries at validation time
_ALL_ENTRIES list contains both operational and theoretical DependencyEntry objects
DEPENDENCY_REGISTRY (public) contains only operational entries
_THEORETICAL_ENTRIES (internal) holds theoretical entries for lookup
Public and internal registries are separated for different use cases
